output.final_deliverable:
Collected links from Wikipedia Main Page (based only on the merged visit result provided)

Source evidence (from merged results):
- Visited URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
- links_count reported: 20
- links_sample reported (3 items):
  1. https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias
  2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
  3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia

Extracted items (3 of 10 requested — remaining items missing from the merged results):

1) URL: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias
   - Link text/title: (inferred from URL) "List of Wikipedias"
   - Brief description: (inferred) A Meta-Wikimedia page that lists the different language editions of Wikipedia and related statistics. 
   - Evidence basis: This URL appears in the visit result's links_sample. (See source: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias)

2) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
   - Link text/title: (inferred from URL) "Wikipedia:Contents"
   - Brief description: (inferred) A Wikipedia internal/portal page that organizes and links to content outlines and entry points for browsing Wikipedia articles by topic and type. 
   - Evidence basis: This URL appears in the visit result's links_sample. (See source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents)

3) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia
   - Link text/title: (inferred from URL) "Wikipedia"
   - Brief description: (inferred) The main encyclopedia article about Wikipedia, describing the project, its history, and principles. 
   - Evidence basis: This URL appears in the visit result's links_sample. (See source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia)

Missing data (required to complete the mandate to 10 links):
- The merged results contain only a sample of links (links_sample of 3 URLs) and a links_count value (20). The full HTML or a complete list of links and in-page link texts/titles and context/descriptions were not included in the merged results.
- Therefore I cannot extract the remaining 7 link URLs, their exact link text/title, or accurate 1-2 sentence descriptions from the provided evidence.

What is needed to finish the task correctly:
- The full list of links (or the full HTML of the Wikipedia Main Page) as retrieved from the visit, or a follow-up visit result that includes the complete set of link URLs and their link text/titles and surrounding context.

Summary of exact values present in the merged result (explicitly listed):
1. Visited page URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2. links_count: 20
3. links_sample[1]: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias
4. links_sample[2]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
5. links_sample[3]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia

Note: I used only the evidence present in the merged results. The request asked for 10 links with titles and descriptions; the merged results only provided 3 sample links, so 7 links and their required details are missing from the provided evidence.
output.action_summary: I visited https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page per the merged results. The visit result reported links_count = 20 and provided a links_sample of 3 URLs: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/List_of_Wikipedias, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents, and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia. I extracted those 3 links (with inferred titles and short inferred descriptions) and listed the exact values reported. The merged results did not include the full list of links or link texts/descriptions, so the remaining 7 links requested are missing from the evidence and cannot be provided without additional page data or a full retrieval.

merged_results.title: Select 10 prominent links from the main page and write 1-2 sentence descriptions

result.title: Select 10 prominent links from the main page and write 1-2 sentence descriptions

details.intent: From the visit result, choose 10 prominent internal links (representing distinct sections of the Main Page) and craft a 1-2 sentence description for each using the main page context_snippet. If the main page provides insufficient context for an accurate description for any chosen link, mark that link for optional deeper visit (not part of this immediate step).
processing_steps: Receive extracted link candidates (url, link_text, context_snippet) from the Visit node.

Prioritize links by prominence (Featured article, Today's featured picture, In the news, On this day, Did you know, Picture of the day, Random article link if visible, Current events, Community portal, About Wikipedia, etc.).
For each selected link, generate a concise 1-2 sentence description using only the context_snippet and any unambiguous metadata on the main page to avoid guessing content that isn't present.

If a context_snippet is absent or ambiguous for a given link, annotate it as 'context limited' and include exactly what minimal follow-up (a direct visit of the linked page) would be needed to complete the description.
measurable_output_fields: results: array of 10 objects { url: string, link_text: string, description: string, source_of_description: 'main_page_snippet' | 'needs_visit' }
verification_steps: Ensure exactly 10 items in results array.
Each description must be 1-2 sentences and must reference information available in the main page's context_snippet (or be flagged 'needs_visit').
No invented claims beyond the main page's text.
details.rationale: A think/processing step ensures the chosen links are representative of the main page and that descriptions are traceable to in-page content. This step organizes the output for clear presentation and flags any links that lack sufficient in-page description for optional follow-up.
details.parent_goal: Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.

ff691055-c7c5-42e2-b5d8-9e48f00c31f1.title: Visit Wikipedia Main Page (retrieve full page and link candidates)

details.intent: Download the full HTML/text of the Wikipedia Main Page so I can extract link URLs, link text, and any in-page summaries or context that will support 1-2 sentence descriptions without necessarily visiting each linked page.
target_facts: All internal link URLs (hrefs) that appear in main content areas (e.g., Featured article, In the news, On this day, Did you know, Picture of the day, Today's featured picture, navigation boxes) — candidates for the 10 links.
Link anchor text / visible title for each candidate link.
Any surrounding text or summary snippets on the main page that describe the linked page (e.g., the featured article blurb).
details.extraction_plan: 1) Download full page content. 2) Parse primary content sections of the main page (Featured content, In the news, On this day, Did you know, Today's featured article/picture, navigation area). 3) Extract internal en.wikipedia.org links within those sections, capturing: exact href, anchor text, and the surrounding sentence/paragraph that provides a short summary or context. 4) Produce a short list of at least 10 candidate links ranked by prominence on the page (e.g., featured article first, then items in In the news, etc.).
measurable_output_fields: candidates: array of objects { url: string, link_text: string, context_snippet: string } (at least 10 items)
verification_steps: Confirm each extracted URL is an en.wikipedia.org URL and that the anchor text is non-empty.
Ensure the context_snippet is taken from within the main page HTML within one HTML block (e.g., the featured article blurb or news bullet) to support the short description.
If fewer than 10 usable internal links are found in primary sections, expand to other main-page areas (sidebar, navigation) until 10 distinct internal links are collected.
details.rationale: Visiting the main page is mandatory to get the authoritative list of prominent links and any in-page summary text that will let me produce accurate 1-2 sentence descriptions without needing to open each linked article. This reduces uncertainty because the main page itself often contains concise summaries for featured items.
details.parent_goal: Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.
details.original_goal: Go to the Wikipedia main page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page) and collect 10 links from the page. For each link, provide: the link URL, the link text/title, and a brief description (1-2 sentences) of what the page is about. Organize the results clearly.
